steps.title: Find the smaller map's side length
steps.content: $70\%$ shorter means the new side length is $30\%$ of the original side length: $$45(0.30)=13.5\text{ inches}.$$

steps.title: Find the actual distance represented
steps.content: The original $45$-inch side represents $$45\cdot13=585$$ miles. The smaller map shows the same region, so its $13.5$-inch side also represents $585$ miles.

steps.title: Compute the new scale
steps.content: The number of miles represented by $1$ inch on the smaller map is $$\frac{585}{13.5}=43.33\ldots.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Rounded to two decimal places, the scale is $43.33$ miles per inch. This matches D.
